application.name: Daily Observation
application.targetHuman: A person who wants to become more attentive to nearby life without maintaining a public nature profile.
application.coreAction: Photograph one living thing and add one sentence about what was actually noticed today.

constraints: Exactly one locally captured or deliberately selected photo belongs to an event.
The sentence must describe a concrete observed detail rather than classify a species.
No network or account is required to capture and seal the observation.

completion.predicate: One image is locally available and the trimmed observation contains one non-empty sentence.

feedback.onProgress: Keep the selected image and unfinished sentence recoverable on the device.
feedback.onCompletion: Show the sealed photo and sentence together once, without scoring the observation.
feedback.onInterruption: Preserve the unfinished observation and offer to resume it without creating a completed day.

runtime.returnInvitation: At the person's chosen local time on another day, invite them to notice one living thing again without shaming a gap.

accumulated.description: A private field notebook grows through distinct observations, seasonal return, and the owner's own words rather than a competitive streak.
projections: places generalized on device when the owner chooses

forbiddenPatterns: species leaderboard or observation streak shame
public map of private photo locations
automatic cloud upload of photos or sentences
dashboard replacing the daily observation action
visualDirection: camera and one-sentence field are the only primary surface
the owner's photograph remains visually primary
seasonal continuity appears as a small local trace, not charts
implementationNotes: Store full-resolution media as immutable encrypted blobs referenced by the event rather than inline JSON.
Strip or preserve image metadata only through an explicit owner-visible policy; do not silently publish location.
The named identity suite is an application contract identifier, not a claim that TOHSENO has selected its first generic cryptographic suite.
The local reflection selects a prior observation by deterministic seasonal policy and never requires AI.

operatorNotes: The client retains source, bundle and package identifiers, store identities, signing assets, and every data plane.
Do not add a server solely to centralize observations.
